message:
fcp-bootstrap/soft_token: store PIN in HardwareTokenPin wrapper and add no-keys scenario

The soft-token test driver used to store the configured PIN as a raw
String field and authenticate inbound sessions by constructing a
fresh HardwareTokenPin on every call and doing `*pin != expected`.
The old in-line comments acknowledged the redaction/visibility
concerns but landed on a roundabout PartialEq check that left the PIN
material sitting in a plain String on the driver struct.

Rework the driver so:

  - SoftTokenDriver::pin is typed HardwareTokenPin, constructed once
    from the SoftTokenConfig::pin at build time.
  - pin() clones the stored HardwareTokenPin instead of re-wrapping a
    String every call.
  - open_authenticated_session compares incoming pins via the
    HardwareTokenPin PartialEq path directly against the stored
    wrapper, keeping comparison on the constant-time route the real
    PKCS#11 driver uses.

This also removes the stale "we can't compare directly" explanatory
block that no longer applies.

Separately, add a new verification-pack scenario
`hwtoken-no-keys` (scenario 8): a NoKeysSoftTokenDriver wraps
SoftTokenDriver, preserves the certificate enumeration behavior of
the inner driver, and returns an empty key list from enumerate_keys.
This drives select_certificate_for_provisioning into the distinct
NoKeys refusal branch of CertificateSelectionRefusal which was
otherwise uncovered by the existing scenarios. The new test captures
setup and negative evidence into VerificationRecord like the rest of
the pack, and the verification_pack imports are updated to include
AuthenticatedTokenSession needed by the wrapper impl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
